spec_match.rationale: The page now describes and tests the actual API surface: four stored rotations plus boolean horizontal flip. It constructs a real VideoFrame and derives the displayed rotation, flip, coded size, display size, and matrix from the frame readback.

interactive.rationale: Rotation and flip controls plus the render button update raw/oriented canvases and the matrix readout.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede no longer overclaims EXIF labels; it promises the 4x2 rotation/flip model that the controls expose.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers quarter-turn display dimension swaps and mirrored matrix composition; arbitrary numeric rotation parsing is handled by the sibling rounding probe.

references.rationale: See-also links include the feature page, constructor demo, WebCodecs spec, and ChromeStatus entry.

design_system.rationale: Uses variables for UI chrome; raw colors are limited to canvas pixels.

summary: Verified in Chrome 148 with chrome-devtools-mcp: selected rotation=270 and flip=true, the page reported coded 320x240, display 240x320, flip=true, a non-crashing matrix readout, and no console issues.
